title: PGT-NeRF: Physics-Guided Thermal Neural Radiance Fields
container-title: Proceedings of the 21st International Conference on Computer Vision Theory and Applications

publisher: Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers (IEEE)
title: SAC-NeRF: Adaptive Ray Sampling for Neural Radiance Fields via Soft Actor-Critic Reinforcement Learning

abstract: <jats:p>Neural Radiance Fields (NeRF) achieve photorealistic novel view synthesis under controlled conditions. By incorporating view directions as input, NeRF can model view-dependent reflective effects. However, this view-dependent property also makes NeRF vulnerable to degraded data, as blur and occlusions unintentionally present during capture can be incorrectly modeled as view-dependent appearance. Unlike previous methods that are designed to address specific types of degradation, we propose an uncertainty-guided NeRF capable of handling diverse degradation factors. Specifically, we introduce a view-consistency constraint to prevent overfitting to view-dependent degraded artifacts. Furthermore, we model the uncertainty of input images to reduce the negative impact of degraded pixels on NeRF. These two components are independent of degradation types and network architectures, enabling improved robustness of existing NeRF-based models under diverse degraded conditions. Experiments on multiple degraded datasets demonstrate the robustness, generalization, and scalability of our method.</jats:p>
title: Uncertainty-guided Neural Radiance Fields against Diverse Degradation Factors
POM-NeRF: Perception-Oriented Modification of Neural Radiance Fields for Enhanced 3D Volumetric Rendering from Multi-View Images
